analysis: Hotspot is OpenMP barrier time driven by load imbalance from periodic heavy iterations. Selected plan is to explicitly control OpenMP scheduling to better distribute heavy iterations and reduce tail effects, replacing schedule(runtime) with a guided schedule and small chunk size. This preserves independence of iterations and reduction semantics; only scheduling policy changes. Numerical sum order may vary across schedules as before; checksum stability expectation remains similar to prior runtime scheduling behavior.
selected_candidate_target: OpenMP parallel for loop scheduling pragma
applied_changes_summary: Changed OpenMP loop scheduling from schedule(runtime) to schedule(guided,4) to improve load balance and reduce barrier wait time while keeping reduction and loop body unchanged.
correctness_risks: Floating-point reduction order differs across schedules/threads, which can slightly change CHECKSUM on some platforms (this risk already exists with schedule(runtime) depending on OMP_SCHEDULE).
Some OpenMP runtimes may perform differently with guided scheduling; if performance regresses, rollback to previous schedule setting.
